economic_notes: Revenue dependency: 56% from government grants (46% in 2022), 31% from fishing revenues (39% in 2022). Subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc. and Érablière Malécite S.E.C. (fully consolidated). Joint ventures (equity method): Salaweg inc. (33%, seaweed processing) and Érablière du Lac de l'Île inc. (50%, maple syrup). Also holds minority interests in Les Crevettiers Marinard inc., 9375-8795 Québec inc. (100% cost), Énergie Éolienne Bas-Saint-Laurent SENC (10%), and 9060-7375 Québec inc. (16.67%). Fishing rights (shrimp, lobster, crab, sea urchins, sea cucumbers) are a major asset.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc. and Érablière Malécite S.E.C. Two joint ventures accounted for by modified equity method (not consolidated): Salaweg inc. (33%) and Érablière du Lac de l'Île inc. (50%). Large deferred revenue balance of $8.69M including $6.13M from DFO for fisheries access. Trust funds (federal government fiduciary funds) of $343,605 held separately. No schedule of remuneration provided. Expenses by segment from Note 25 used for sectoral breakdown; 'Développement économique' segment includes commercial activities totaling $5.42M in expenses. Dûs sur les immobilisations corporelles of $876,777 included in liabilities. Accounting modification of $434,282 applied to prior year opening surplus. Provincial transfers include large Tourisme Québec deferred amount ($824,776) and Parcs Canada ($827,562).
